hook: Bob, your classroom and your mortgage are on the same ballot—and the margin that decides them is smaller than your school's graduating class.

because.text: you should vote because the Lieutenant Governor race was decided by just 399,125 votes (5.3%) in 2022, and that office controls which education bills even reach a vote—including voucher expansion that could redirect funding from your public school

you should vote because Texas has the nation's highest uninsured rate, and the governor's refusal to expand Medicaid has contributed to rural hospital closures that affect healthcare access for uninsured teachers like you

source.title: Texas’ next top lawyer: What does the attorney general do and how has Paxton remade the office?

because.text: you should vote because candidates diverge 90 points on inflation policy—one supports anti-gouging and anti-monopoly intervention that could affect your cost of living, while the other pursues spending cuts and deregulation

source.title: Bust Monopolies Petition - Clayton Tucker for TX Ag Commissioner

because.text: you should vote because only 45.7% of registered voters turned out in 2022, meaning non-voters effectively handed control of the Legislative Budget Board—which writes the state budget—to the candidates they didn't choose

if_you_vote: Your single vote joins a pool where 399,125 votes decided who controls the Texas Senate agenda—including whether ESA voucher expansion reaches the floor, affecting your public school's funding. On inflation, you're choosing between anti-monopoly intervention and reduced regulation approaches that diverge 90 points. The governor's line-item veto power over the state budget, decided by margins under 900,000 votes, determines whether Medicaid expansion reaches a million uninsured Texans like you.
long_term: The 2026 governor's race branches into two stark paths: continued Medicaid non-expansion and ESA voucher growth under Abbott, or expansion and $15,000 teacher raises under Hinojosa—though either faces Republican legislative supermajorities. Your vote in 2026 compounds into 2028 redistricting battles, 2030 census-driven funding formulas for your school district, and whether Texas maintains its uninsured crisis or begins to close the coverage gap that affects your healthcare access as a teacher.
cta: Make a plan to vote November 3, 2026—check your registration at votetexas.gov and calendar your school's election day.
